summary: DHH promotes an upcoming talk by Thomas Madsen-Mygdal at Copenhagen Business School about entrepreneurship. He enthusiastically endorses Mygdal as an 'entrepreneur extraordinaire' and encourages readers to attend the seminar. The post is a brief, informal announcement typical of early 2000s blogging, with a playful suggestion that non-students fake their way in. It reflects DHH's early Danish tech community connections before his rise to international prominence.
key_points: Thomas Madsen-Mygdal, associated with Reboot and Common, will speak at Copenhagen Business School about entrepreneurship
The seminar covers foundational entrepreneurial questions about spirit, meaning, and demands of the lifestyle
DHH playfully encourages non-CBS students to attend by posing as first-year HA.dat students
money_quotes: You'll need to fake being a student of CBS, but that shouldn't be too hard. Just say you're from HA.dat first year. I'll vouch for you.
key_insight: A brief early-blogging-era snapshot of DHH's enthusiasm for the Danish entrepreneurial community and his irreverent, rule-bending personality.
